file_level.general_notes: Best practices score: 10/10 (0 FAIL, 0 WARN, 6 PASS)

rules.detail: The file is syntactically valid JSON. No trailing commas, no comments, all keys are properly double-quoted, and all values use correct JSON types (strings and booleans).

rules.rule_name: No hardcoded secrets, passwords, or API keys
rules.detail: The file contains only TypeScript compiler options. No sensitive data, credentials, tokens, or API keys are present.

All keys consistently use camelCase, which is the standard TypeScript/tsconfig convention: compilerOptions, moduleResolution, esModuleInterop, skipLibCheck, forceConsistentCasingInFileNames, resolveJsonModule, isolatedModules, noEmit. No mixing with snake_case or other styles.
Maximum nesting depth is 2 levels (root → compilerOptions → option values; root → include → array items), well within the 5-level limit. Evaluated leniently per tsconfig.json rules.

All keys are fully descriptive TypeScript compiler option names. No single-letter keys or cryptic abbreviations are present. Keys like forceConsistentCasingInFileNames and isolatedModules are self-documenting.
The file uses consistent 2-space indentation throughout all 15 lines. It is not minified and is well-structured with clear visual hierarchy separating compilerOptions from include.
